meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Learn how to master portfolio building and overcome debugging hurdles. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your dev career.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of process. By shifting from 'fixing code' to 'building portfolio-worthy systems,' you force yourself to architect modular, testable components. Use Antigravity for automated logic verification and Claude Code for iterative refinement, turning every bug into a documented feature of your professional portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a cryptic stack trace, the cursor blinking like a mocking heartbeat. You’ve been here for hours, lost in a labyrinth of dependency hell and logic loops, feeling the imposter syndrome creep in. You aren't failing; you are simply hitting the invisible wall that every senior developer once faced before they learned the art of systematic engineering.

True mastery isn't about writing bug-free code—it's about building a robust ecosystem where bugs are isolated, understood, and eliminated. By focusing on portfolio building, you transform your development environment into a laboratory. This transition from 'student coder' to 'systems architect' is exactly what separates the hobbyists from the engineers who get hired.

steps.title: Architecting for Observability
steps.description: Before you write code, you must design for failure. Structure your project into decoupled services so that bugs are contained in manageable, testable units.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project architecture and suggest a modular structure that isolates business logic from UI, specifically highlighting where to inject logging for debugging.

steps.title: Automated Logic Verification
steps.description: Use Antigravity to verify your core logic paths before integration, ensuring that your foundation is mathematically sound.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify the logic flow of this authentication module. Identify potential edge cases where the promise might reject silently.

steps.title: Iterative Refinement with Claude Code
steps.description: Don't just fix a bug; iterate on the component. Use Claude Code to refactor the broken section while maintaining the existing API contracts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this component to handle async errors gracefully, ensuring it meets production-grade standards for my portfolio.

steps.title: Documenting the 'Why' for Portfolio
steps.description: Every fix is a story. Use your terminal to generate technical documentation that explains the 'why' behind your architectural decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the debugging process for this feature, focusing on the architectural challenge and the technical solution for a portfolio case study.

steps.title: Integration Testing for Reliability
steps.description: Finalize the build by running an integration suite that tests how your modules interact under stress, proving your app is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests that simulate high-concurrency requests to ensure the fix in step 3 holds under pressure.

pro_tips: Always treat your commit history as a professional narrative; squash bugs into descriptive, feature-focused commits.
Use 'Rubber Duck Debugging' via Claude Code by explaining the logic out loud while the AI acts as the listener.
Maintain a 'Failure Log' in your portfolio; hiring managers value the raw honesty of how you solved a production-level outage.
Build 'Test-First' habits; if you can't write a test for it, you don't understand the feature well enough to build it yet.
Prioritize readability over brevity; code is read 100x more than it is written, especially by your future self.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, push it to GitHub, write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post, and share it in our community channel to get peer code reviews.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs define your limits. Start building systems that prove your expertise and land you the interviews you deserve. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
